top_categories.workout_context: 994 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
The Accursed Race (TAR)'s mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 994 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic The Accursed Race (TAR)'s demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
At The Accursed Race (TAR), surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
121,391ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
122ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
121,391ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 994 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
994 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 121,391ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At The Accursed Race (TAR), that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of The Accursed Race (TAR). Train it until it feels boring.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 994 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 994 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at The Accursed Race (TAR). Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at The Accursed Race (TAR) demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride mixed terrain at race-day cadence weekly. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 994 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Gravel Specific, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 994 miles of mixed terrain in Albania/Montenegro/Bosnia and Herzegovina/Kosovo.
